statement.markdown:
In this problem, you are given two strings. The goal is to find the length of the longest common subsequence between these two strings. The string is 0-indexed.

Your function will take two parameters: `text1` and `text2`. You can use any extra space to store your memoization table.

You should return the length of the longest common subsequence.
statement.inputFormat: Two strings `text1` and `text2` will be provided as input.
statement.outputFormat: An integer `length` representing the length of the longest common subsequence.
statement.notes: The function can return any integer type.

examples.explanation: The longest common subsequence between BANANA and FOXDANCE is ANA
The longest common subsequence between ABCD and CDEA is AD
The longest common subsequence between AAA and AAB is AA

editorial.title: Incorrect Memoization in Subsequence
editorial.intuition: To solve this problem, we can use memoization to store intermediate values.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a 2D array dp to store the length of the longest common subsequence for each substring of text1 and text2.
